sections.title: 1. What is a Landlocked Country?
points: A landlocked country is a country that is entirely surrounded by land and has no direct access to the sea or ocean.
Africa has 16 landlocked countries, which is more than any other continent.
Because they lack their own sea ports, these countries must rely on their coastal neighbors for international trade.

sections.title: 2. Regional List of Landlocked Nations
points: East Africa: Uganda, Rwanda, Burundi, and South Sudan.
The Horn: Ethiopia (became landlocked in 1993 after Eritrea separated).
West Africa (Sahel): Mali, Niger, and Burkina Faso.
Central Africa: Chad and Central African Republic (CAR).
Southern Africa: Zambia, Zimbabwe, Malawi, Botswana, Eswatini, and Lesotho.

sections.title: 3. Vital Trade Routes and Ports
points: Uganda: Uses the 'Northern Corridor' to reach the Port of Mombasa in Kenya.
Rwanda & Burundi: Use the 'Central Corridor' to reach the Port of Dar es Salaam in Tanzania.
Ethiopia: Uses the Port of Djibouti (connected by a modern railway).
Mali: Relies on the Port of Dakar in Senegal.
Zambia: Uses Dar es Salaam (via TAZARA railway) and Durban in South Africa.
Zimbabwe: Uses the Port of Beira in Mozambique (its shortest route to the sea).

sections.title: 4. Major Challenges faced by Landlocked Countries
points: High Transport Costs: Goods are expensive because they travel long distances through multiple countries.
Dependence: If a neighboring coastal country has a strike or civil war, the landlocked country's trade is blocked.
Delays: Goods spend a long time at borders for clearing and taxes.
Smuggling: Illegal trade is common due to many porous land borders.

sections.title: 5. Special Case: The Enclave State
points: An Enclave is a country that is completely surrounded by only one other country.
Lesotho: The most famous enclave in Africa, located entirely inside South Africa.
Lesotho is highly dependent on South Africa for all its electricity, transport, and economic needs.

sections.text: PLE Exam Fact: Ethiopia is the most populated landlocked country in the world, while South Sudan is the newest landlocked nation in Africa (2011).

cards.fact: A country surrounded by only one neighbor.
cards.mnemonic: Enclave = Enclosed like a letter in an envelope.

cards.fact: The inland region far from the coast that a port serves.
The railway connecting landlocked Zambia to the Tanzanian port of Dar es Salaam.
The transport route from landlocked Uganda/Rwanda to Mombasa port.
